reviews.review_description: Why did it have to end like this, it is so peak.

This is a pretty fun puzzle arcade game. Moss is much better. I like the Japanese myth and folklore theme mixed with steampunk. I also liked the Edo era towns you go through which are very authentic.  I thought it was a little lazy to bring back the five bosses for another battle that is exactly the same but a little harder. They should have just gone to the final battle. TvSome TV elements of the tasks were dependent on color, which is difficult doe colorblind people like me. If a challenge requires color, be sure to use blue. It was a long playtime. But overall fun game.

I was genuinely getting into Haru as a character—until she dry fired a compound bow right in front of me. It’s clear she never took her miko training seriously. That bow is toast. No amount of sacred energy is fixing shattered cams and frayed cables. If this is the level of care she puts into divine equipment, I’m not sure she should be trusted with the fate of the world.

That said, I’ve got to give credit for the little details—the sound of the shackle when she moves her leg is a really nice touch.

The idea is solid, you have an action puzzle game with a selection of bosses you have to guide an NPC around and protect. If the devlopers had let a single tester near it it could be good. As is though the puzzles are simply "Read the developers mind, there are no clues" and the bosses involve getting split second timing to block attacks when your controls have literally tens of seconds of lag involved. This is because you have to synchronise with the NPC you are protecting and her animations take ten to twenty seconds to play out. The attacks you have to block are telegraphed about 5 seconds in advance so you have to know in advance what the enemy will be doing to time your blocks perfectly. Given trial and error it can be done but the things you are doing are also trial and error and the result is simply miserable. On top of this the game looks awful, with anything outside of a pinpoint lens directly in front of you shown at unbelievably low resolution.
Just wanted to say that I just beat the game, and I absolutely loved it! I loved the story and everything, and possibly looking forward to a part two? :3

Haru, the main character, is absolutely adorable and I loved her from the start. The puzzles were challenging (I know I got a few headaches along the way!) but they were always solvable without needing to fallback on a walkthrough. The story was great and I got really emotionally invested!
I’d absolutely love a sequel where we go back to help Haru again, with more ways to communicate with her this time! Maybe voice recognition?

Love the game but something about it majorly makes me motion sick.  It hits quickly and I have to exit the game.   I have tried multiple times with the same results so I have uninstalled it.  Sad I can not play it.  The game is beautiful and love the abilities.  Just can’t play it.

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing. We provide settings for movement speed, warp movement, and movement effects in the options. We hope you will try the settings that are just right for you and less motion sickness.

reviews.review_description:
I actually finished this game around a month ago. Have been busy and unable to write a review until now. I really enjoyed this game from start to finish. The puzzles and enemies start off easy so you can get a feel for things. As you progress the enemies and puzzles get more intense. The boss battles and animation is beautiful. What I enjoyed most about the game is the connection I felt with the main character. Throughout your journey the both of you need to work together to accomplish many objectives. She is very talkative and encouraging throughout the game. After going through many battles and puzzles you really start to feel a connection to the character as if your really in that world. This game was meant for VR and I'm glad I had a chance to experience it. 

Any chance of a sequel.....

reviews.review_title: Poor game for a motion sick people.
reviews.review_description: The game is really beautiful and entertaining. But there is one major game breaking problem that It's impossible for me to move the stone and walk at the same time using the teleportation movement and it's the only way that doesn't make me nauseous. The second boss fight is too hard to win that way. I had to abandon the game cause i couldn't progress further.

developer_response.body:
We are sorry about that, Victor_Voyce. You may try moving the stone forward or backward by pressing the A/B or X/Y button of the hand you are grabbing the stone, if it helps relieve the problem.
If the stone is near the destination of the teleportation and blocks the movement, trying to hold the stone a little bit higher may help.
Also, we are working on an update which provides an option to adjust the battle difficulty, in response to feedback from players. Please try again when we release the update.

reviews.review_description:
The Tale of Onogoro has all you need
Puzzles
Shooting
Hidden things to find and shoot
Levels
Japanese temples
Monsters
Giant monsters
Cute steam punk priestess who rides a magic rock
Holding hands
Vague 4th wall breaking dialogue

reviews.review_title: It’s good she just talks a lot
reviews.review_description: I’ve only played for about an hour and a half but it’s fun. I was anticipating this game because it seemed like something I would enjoy playing. I wasn’t wrong but it could be better. I’ll update when I finish the game, hopefully it takes me a while and has a good replay ability.

reviews.review_title: Love it...but could be better if–
reviews.review_description:
I love the game. The story, the settings and the characters. 





But dang! Please let me headpat Haru, she's adorable! I was pretty disappointed that I couldn't interact with her other then occasionally hold her hand whenever she needed to deactivate a barrier during the end of each level. Besides that, my hands always pass through her!

If she only acknowledged my attempts to headpat her for a job well done or whenever she says or does something adorable. Then I'll be even more a happy player.

reviews.review_title: I want to headpat the shrine maiden
reviews.review_description: Please add more interactions like covering Haru's mouth to skip dialogue or headpatting Haru. The puzzles, design, and gameplay are great. This game is definitely worthy of 5 stars and worth every cent.

This game makes you feel like you’re in a fun anime, and your companion Haru is super cute and gives you hints as you solve puzzles to get through different maps in your journey to help her. It’s super rewarding to solve these puzzles - particularly the harder ones - which open up doors to get your further along. The graphics are absolutely stunning, and the celestial weapons you use are pretty sweet. It’s very visually pleasing to play. There’s lots of action when fighting kami, as taking them down is not easy and Haru helps you figure out how to defeat them. I love that you get to answer Haru with head nods and hold hands with her. You will build a bond with the character as your companion! The game can be played sitting or standing (although standing is fun when you battle kami and need to duck and dodge their attacks). Yes Haru can talk a lot but it’s part of the narrative as the game is in fact telling a tale. If you’re looking for a chill game with awesome graphics, a storyline, puzzles and action then this game is for you. It’s a nice mix of everything. I haven’t finished the game yet but can’t wait to continue.

Just started playing and it is still a good game but I was expecting more visually, areas seem static and penned in. Definitely could improve the dubbing. Puzzles could be more diverse using more hand mechanics then your point and click guns.  Biggest grip would be the scoring at end of each area loses immersion and gives a cellphone game vibe I could do without. Still charming and fun. Worth a play but more $20 than $30 seems about right.
Update: puzzles got more complex but still overuse of celestial weapons mechanic.  Lightning kami boss is hard because moving her around is too slow to keep her and you from getting hit. Trying to healing Her during this time takes too long when she even holds her hands out.the red area warning on haru is pointless as picking her up takes more time then the attack hits. This fight needs better balancing.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your comments. Your feedback will be taken into consideration for future reference.
As the game progresses, there will be more challenging puzzles and maybe surprises! Hope you would enjoy it till the end.

reviews.review_description: Too much talking and explaining without the option to skip dialogue.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your opinion.
Event dialogue can be skipped by opening the Options Menu during an event and selecting "Skip Event." Please feel free to make use of it.
I hope this helps!

reviews.review_title: possible to skip too much talking
reviews.review_description:
you can skip Haru's infinite dialogue in option when you have to play the same scene again. Don't give 2 stars grade because of that. But I also admit Haru is a too much talker, so I can't give 5 stars to Onogoro, but the other parts of game is great and well made

Remember Masatake

He is a main villain, and the same name existed as a villain in Korea history. In 1910 Japanese empire forcefully colonized Chonsun dynasty. At that time Terauchi Masatake played an important role to steal the national sovereignty of Chosun. So he became the first Japanese Governor-General of Korea. Masatake? really suitable name...

게임 악당이름이 마사타케인데 1910년 한일합방 주도해서 초대 조선총독이 된 사람이 테라우치 마사타케임. 잘 어울리는 이름

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your support, we are happy to hear that you enjoyed the game and its characters!
I'd just like to note that Arakida Masatake is not modelled after a specific individual and is not related in any way to Terauchi Masatake or other real persons. There is also no political message within The Tale of Onogoro. 

아라키다 마사타케에는 특정한 인물을 모티브하지않은 오리지널 캐릭터로써  테라우치 마사타케와는 일절 관계가 없습니다. 또한, 오노고로 이야기는 정치적인 메시지를 담고 있지 않습니다.

reviews.review_description: I had to update my first review. Beautiful scenery and challenging tasks to perform.  Sitting may not be ideal to complete the whole game.  The developers are very responsive and engaging to make sure there are no bugs or bad experiences.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your opinion.
We believe the point of 2 windmills you mentioned is in Map 3 of Chapter 2. It is indeed one of the tricky parts that Haru is separated apart and cannot provide clues to the player. If you pay attention to the windmills, one of them has a little bit different style. Maybe you can trying shooting it with amplified Ki and see how it goes, just like how you did to the amplified Fire switch in the previous area. You also need to think of a way to absorb all 3 Wind Kis in the area with your 2 Celestial Weapons in order to clear the area.
Hope you solve the puzzle and continue playing till the end!

However, if you ever think that you encountered a bug, we would appreciate it if you contact us at:
https://onogoro.info/en/contact/

reviews.review_description: It doesn't literally have portal gun gameplay. But it is a physics based environmental puzzle game in the vein of Portal. Great utilization of VR mechanics for intense moments, like when you heal by holding hands with Haru while keeping an eye on the boss who usually will prepare and also execute their next attack before you can recover all your health. The game really nails all of its gameplay ingredients, resulting in an almost perfect VR action adventure. In the top 2 of best VR games released in 2022 so far.

The retro-Japanese setting and the characters may look nothing like Portal, but the game has a lot of things in common: gravity-gun puzzles interwoven with storytelling, alongside a likable sidekick character. Haru, the player’s companion, is a LOT more talkative than Katia, the character in Amata’s previous game Last Labyrinth. She plays a part of the worldbuilding by telling you all about the game’s alternate-history Japan, and will also play a part in assisting the player to get through the puzzles and boss fights. The graphics, audio, and acting are all top notch. Highly recommended!
